evidence: docs/README.md defines explicit lifecycle routes and says historical, public, and binary evidence must not be moved or deleted without an approved retention manifest.

The local HGI SPEC requires public docs, completed history, failed or inconclusive evidence, and binary artifacts to remain separately routed and retained until an approved manifest authorizes movement or deletion.
The active HGI plan records that HGI-200 preserved public content, binary evidence, and historical plans in place.
rejectedAlternatives: Physically move implemented specs, completed plans, public docs, or binary evidence as part of HGI-207.
Delete or collapse retained historical, public, failed, inconclusive, or binary evidence after lifecycle classification.
Treat a decision record as an approved retention manifest.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: Use strict lifecycle routing while retaining existing paths in place. Physical archive movement or deletion is unavailable unless a separately approved retention manifest names the targets, authority, retention basis, rollback or recovery, and readback.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: Lifecycle routing does not make retained public content current public truth.
Retained paths do not establish release-proof retention policy.
Runbooks cannot authorize archive movement without a retention manifest.
Controls may enforce routing but cannot authorize deletion or movement.

authorityImpact: This accepted local default preserves existing evidence and grants no deletion, movement, publication, registry, provider, or deployment authority.
nonClaims: Strict lifecycle routing does not prove the historical implementation, public availability, release, or consumer behavior of a retained artifact.
No archive, history, public content, binary artifact, or evidence was moved or deleted.
carryingCost: Maintain links and lifecycle metadata at retained paths; review any proposed retention manifest against the named evidence classes.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: A proposed retention manifest names a bounded target set, named authority, retention basis, recovery path, and readback for physical movement or deletion.
